findings.title: First global cold-start beach-bacteria forecast beats published transfer SOTA
findings.headline: 37 regions · leave-one-region-out AUC 0.726 vs recurrence 0.69 · beats recurrence in 34/37 · +rain -> 0.746
findings.plain: One pooled model, trained on many regions and tested on a held-out region it never saw ('cold start'), forecasts which beaches will breach the bacteria standard across 37 European and US regions. It beats the region's own history-repeat baseline ('recurrence') and edges the best published cross-region transfer score; adding rainfall as a lead-time input lifts it further.
findings.how: Leave-one-region-out (train on all regions but one, test on the held-out one) across 37 regions; macro-averaged AUC and average precision (AP) versus a recurrence baseline and versus the published external transfer SOTA; rainfall lead-time added as a separate test.
findings.note: Scope: this is a cold-start RANKING win for regions/beaches with little local history. Where a region's own recent history is rich, recurrence is still competitive; the model's edge is generalizing to places it has never seen.

findings.title: Cold-start surge-onset model transfers zero-shot across 17 ocean basins
findings.headline: 17/17 basins · leak-hardened +0.09 AP over persistence (raw +0.19)
findings.plain: The same cold-start recipe used for bacteria carries to a completely different hazard: coastal storm-surge onset. Tide-gauge water levels are 'detided' (the predictable astronomical tide removed) to leave the surge residual, and a pooled model predicts surge-onset days on ocean basins it was not trained on. It beats a hardened persistence baseline in all 17 basins tested — orthogonal evidence for the whole cross-hazard thesis.
findings.how: Detide UHSLC gauges with utide -> surge residual; 95th-percentile onset target; leave-one-basin-out. Red-team attacks strip seasonal-envelope leakage (minimal-feature, causal thresholds, and a high-pass detrend), and the honest claim is the leak-hardened margin, not the raw one.
findings.note: Honest framing: the raw +0.19 AP over-credited a seasonal envelope; the claimable, leak-hardened marginal skill is ~+0.09 AP, still separated from zero. Surge is orthogonal to the biological 'one-system' frame — a genuinely new hazard, not a relabel.

findings.title: Shipped: one cold-start model serves an ungauged beach on day one
findings.headline: cold-start ranking lift AUC +0.0968 CI[0.0686, 0.1317] · 5/5 folds · serves 34% a local model can't fit
findings.plain: The cold-start research win is now a single deployable model. When a brand-new or ungauged beach has no local sampling history, distance-weighted neighbor exceedance plus recurrence still give a real day-1 warning ranking; the model auto-sharpens as local samples accrue, and it is calibrated for deployment. It covers the roughly one-third of beaches a history-hungry local model cannot fit.
findings.how: At prediction time the local-memory columns are dropped to missing; neighbor_rate is a distance-weighted prior-month exceedance from K=8 training beaches >=8 km away. Ranking is scored on raw scores on held-out cold folds; isotonic calibration is evaluated on a disjoint half of beaches.
findings.note: The ranking win is specifically on cold / data-poor beaches; where local history is rich it ties a well-powered local model rather than beating it.

findings.title: Training on the real advisory label beats the threshold proxy on unseen beaches
findings.headline: unseen-station dAP +0.1091 CI[0.0504, 0.1772] perm-p 0.02 · proxy worse than recurrence
findings.plain: For the decision that actually matters operationally — will a beach advisory be posted — training on the real posted-advisory label beats training on the usual threshold-exceedance PROXY, on beaches held out of training. The proxy-trained model is actually worse than the simple recurrence baseline. A phase-1 time-holdout signal was first killed as a lab-lag phantom, so this is the surviving, gate-hardened result.
findings.how: Unseen-station + forward-time phantom-hunt gate: train on some beaches, test on 180 held-out stations (56.7k rows); AP of the operational-label model vs the proxy-label model and vs a baseline, with cluster CIs, a permutation null, and a within-station shuffle phantom guard.
findings.note: Action: retrain the deployed FIB advisory on the posted-advisory label. An earlier phase-1 time-holdout signal was first killed as a lab-lag phantom, so this unseen-station result is the surviving one.

findings.title: Bacteria nowcast beats station-memory status quo
findings.headline: AP 0.4992 vs 0.2782 · dAP +0.221
findings.plain: On the EXCLUDE_SAN_DIEGO 2022+ deployable stratum, the calibrated bacteria model beats the station-memory baseline with a clustered-bootstrap AP margin whose CI excludes zero.
findings.how: Frozen temporal holdout, n=89,321 / 8,999 events, compared against a station-memory baseline and checked by decision-curve net benefit.
findings.note: This is the stronger public bacteria headline than the stale pooled/Monterey-only table row.

findings.title: Harmonic tide-range claim card is blocked by source-data gaps
findings.headline: DATA-GAP
findings.plain: The current tide-range card is not promoted as a positive claim because the harmonic-range cache and beach-to-gauge map are not materialized.
findings.how: Claim-card readout records the missing inputs instead of reporting a lift.

reasons: harmonic-range cache present=False; beach->gauge map present=False — both required to materialize TIDE_RANGE_FEATS; rebuild via research/bacteria/tide_range_harmonic_gate.build_harmonic_range

findings.note: Rebuild the harmonic range features before re-testing this candidate.

findings.title: HAB exceedance forecast from prior/as-of ocean and HAB state
findings.headline: AP 0.2571 vs persistence 0.1415; dAP +0.1156 CI [0.0468, 0.265]; perm-p 0.005
findings.plain: On the chronological holdout, a leakage-guarded model predicts HAB pDA exceedance better than the stronger of persistence and seasonal baselines.
findings.how: chronological holdout at 2021-05-10 17:55:00+00:00; train/test rows 4002/1705 with 157/51 events. Best baseline is persistence; dAP CI excludes zero: True; permutation null passes: True. Source table: lakehouse/silver/hab/hab_sota_panel.parquet.
findings.note: Not deployment-ready. This is a retrospective temporal holdout across existing HAB stations; next proof is a frozen prospective/as-of replay and a leave-station/region stress test.

findings.title: Gulf hypoxia flag forecast audit (effort-confounded; not promoted)
findings.headline: Effort-confound control supersedes the apparent AP lift
findings.plain: The earlier chronological Gulf survey-cast flag appeared to beat persistence, but a later effort-confound control tied persistence to an effort placebo. The Gulf lane is retained as an audit caveat/null context, not as a promoted forecast.
findings.how: chronological holdout at 2016-06-10 00:00:00+00:00; train/test rows 3475/1277 with 332/97 events. Best baseline is persistence; dAP CI excludes zero: True; permutation null passes: True. Source table: lakehouse/silver/external_curated/gulf_hypoxia_watch/gulf_hypoxia_watch.parquet.
findings.note: Superseded: sampling density/effort confounds the apparent survey-cast skill, and the interannual dead-zone severity campaign was archived null (promotable_candidates=0). No Gulf forecaster is claimed.

card_read.means: The Gulf survey-cast card is no longer a win; its apparent skill tracks sampling effort rather than a usable forecast signal.
card_read.matters: It stays in the ledger so the public site records why this tempting dead-zone story was stopped.
card_read.careful: The supported low-oxygen lead result is the Tokyo Bay stratification/onset lane; no Gulf deployment or transfer claim is made.

findings.title: Non-bacteria night run promoted HAB; later Gulf hypoxia control downgraded the survey flag
findings.headline: HAB promoted; Gulf retained as caveat after effort-control audit
findings.plain: The original night-run bundle promoted HAB pDA exceedance and a Gulf hypoxia survey flag. The later audit keeps HAB as the supported claim but downgrades the Gulf card because its apparent skill did not survive an effort-confound control.
findings.how: Claim-card critic over the non-bacteria night-run bundle, enforcing chronological holdout, stronger baseline, bootstrap CI, permutation null, and leakage controls.
findings.note: Use the Tokyo Bay hypoxia onset/stratification cards for the supported low-oxygen lead story.

findings.title: Clean-to-dirty beach warning catches surprise flips
findings.headline: 2.1x station-memory on prior-clean beach-days
findings.plain: The hard case is a beach that looked clean, then suddenly turns dirty. On that slice, the model ranks risky flips about twice as well as station memory.
findings.how: Same frozen bacteria model, EXCLUDE_SAN_DIEGO, reveal-lag discipline, evaluated on the onset slice instead of the already-dirty persistence slice.
findings.note: This is a retrospective target-reframe claim. The separate physics-pi/tensor explainer is corrected: physics-pi survives, tensor is not promoted, and the post-2026-06-18 prospective lockbox still needs scoring.

card_read.means: The hard case is a beach that looked clean, then suddenly turns dirty. The model catches those surprise flips about twice as well as station memory.
card_read.matters: That is the warning people cannot get just by asking what happened yesterday.
card_read.careful: Physics-pi is the surviving booster; the tensor add-on is not promoted, and a prospective lockbox still needs scoring.

findings.title: Adaptive sampling replay beats site-memory sampling
findings.headline: top-20 capture 0.7062 vs 0.5299
findings.plain: Under the same sampling budget, model-ranked sampling catches more exceedances than station-memory ranking in retrospective replay.
findings.how: Ranking replay and full mixed-arm weekly protocol replay on frozen bacteria scores.
findings.note: Ops-validation ready; not yet a standalone prospective discovery claim.

findings.title: Bacteria breakthrough gate is retrospective-pass, prospective-blocked
findings.headline: PASS · PASS_INITIAL_PROSPECTIVE_LOCKBOX_LIMITED_N
findings.plain: The operational, onset, and spatial checks pass retrospectively, but the final breakthrough label is blocked by an empty prospective lockbox.
findings.how: Breakthrough gate reads FROZEN_HEADLINE plus onset and spatial holdout metrics, then enforces the post-freeze lockbox rule.
findings.note: FROZEN_HEADLINE.json scored_forward=true after reports/bacteria/prospective_scorecard.json scored HOLDS on 198 post-freeze rows / 8 events. Scoped as an initial prospective shadow-pilot hold; not a production advisory replacement.

findings.title: CatBoost has a real ranking edge over frozen HGBT
findings.headline: AP 0.5164 vs 0.5082 · CI [0.0041, 0.0193]
findings.plain: The CatBoost champion-upgrade candidate beats the frozen HGBT ranking metric with a clustered-bootstrap margin that excludes zero.
findings.how: Same EXCLUDE_SAN_DIEGO temporal holdout; decision-curve analysis then checks whether the ranking edge changes operations.
findings.note: RECORD-DON'T-SHIP (operationally) — the AP margin is real and county-robust (clustered CI [0.0041, 0.0193] excludes 0), but at the advisory threshold the net-benefit gain is NEGLIGIBLE (max |delta| 0.00138 < 0.002). The ranking edge is concentrated in the dry/summer slices but does not translate into deployment value, so a frozen-champion swap + new dependency is not justified on operational grounds. The honest case to ship rests on calibration (ECE) + the dry/summer-slice gain, not net benefit.

findings.title: LightGBM and CatBoost both clear the paired HGBT test
findings.headline: CatBoost +0.0132; LightGBM +0.0081
findings.plain: Two independent tree learners beat frozen HGBT in paired AP tests, confirming the model ceiling is slightly above the old HGBT run.
findings.how: Paired bootstrap plus leave-one-county-out win counts on the same 89,321-row deployable stratum.
findings.note: Registry still keeps them as benchmark/champion-upgrade candidates pending freeze/reproduction and operational-value gates.

findings.title: Frozen bacteria model passes held-from-fitting 2026 season
findings.headline: AP 0.6335 vs station-memory 0.3682
findings.plain: On the locked 2026-01-05 to 2026-03-05 held-from-fitting season, the frozen model beats the strongest baseline and retains discrimination.
findings.how: D3 holdout keys are hash-pinned; F1 scores the frozen model against persistence, seasonal, and station-memory baselines.
findings.note: These 2026-01-05..2026-03-05 rows are inside the frozen TEST split (>=2022) and were ALREADY SCORED by the published headline; they were never trained/calibrated/gated on. This is a HELD-FROM-FITTING forward season, not a pristine never-scored lockbox. The pristine post-2026-06-18 lockbox is EMPTY (data ends 2026-03-05).

findings.title: Bacteria forecast horizon is honest to about 2 days
findings.headline: skill over station-memory through lead 2d; lead 3d loses
findings.plain: The corrected horizon claim compares against a multi-feature station-memory model, not the weak single-bit persistence baseline.
findings.how: Lead-day sweep with reveal-lag discipline and paired AP margin over station-memory model.
findings.note: Current per-lead rows show L=2 is CI-separated over station-memory; L=3 is a loss. Do not claim a 3-day horizon.

findings.title: Deployable bacteria advisory rule catches most exceedances
findings.headline: recall 0.8645 · precision 0.2301
findings.plain: The calibrated decision rule turns the model into an advisory threshold chosen on validation, then read on the 2022+ test era.
findings.how: Validation-chosen threshold tau, then test operating-point and decision-curve readout.
findings.note: No fabricated dollar/illness counts. The trade-off is stated in operational units (exceedances caught vs false advisories) + the DCA-implied cost ratio. A site that wants fewer closures raises tau (toward the max-precision point); a site prioritising public health lowers it (toward max-detection). EPA recreational-water practice errs toward warning, i.e. a LOW tau, which this rule reflects.

findings.title: Russian River upstream turbidity adds lead-2 skill
findings.headline: dAP +0.0489 vs persistence
findings.plain: Upstream turbidity plus downstream persistence beats downstream persistence alone for the Guerneville intake turbidity event target.
findings.how: Leave-year-style out-of-fold lead sweep over Hopland/Digger Bend/Guerneville USGS IV turbidity.
findings.note: A narrow advection escape, not a general bacteria-beach driver claim.

findings.title: Cache Slough upstream lead sweep finds one new escape
findings.headline: dAP +0.1075 at lead 1d
findings.plain: A systematic USGS-IV upstream/downstream sweep found one new turbidity advection escape beyond the Russian River positive control.
findings.how: Screen thousands of gauge pairs, then confirm top candidates with bootstrap and permutation gates.
findings.note: Rare/special-case advection escape; possible tidal-phase artifact remains a caveat.

findings.title: Drivers matter on wet-tail bacteria extremes
findings.headline: wet-tail AP gain +0.0596
findings.plain: Although drivers do not improve the pooled mean, they add strong, CI-separated skill on the wet/storm high-consequence tail.
findings.how: Driver-free versus driver-augmented HGBT contrast on exogenous train-derived p90 severity regimes.
findings.note: This is a tail-only claim; pooled AP slightly regresses.

findings.title: GRU sequence model beats HGBT at 14-28 day hypoxia lead
findings.headline: 14d dAP +0.0315; 28d +0.0292
findings.plain: A genuine temporal GRU extracts multi-week hypoxia lead dynamics that the stratification HGBT misses at 14 and 28 days.
findings.how: Train <=2021, test >2021; station-block/bootstrap deltas against HGBT-strat and persistence.
findings.note: 7d and 21d are not CI-separated against HGBT; report the horizon-specific result.

findings.title: Hypoxia onset forecast beats better naive across horizons
findings.headline: 7d AP 0.835 vs naive 0.7474
findings.plain: On the currently-non-hypoxic origins, the stratification model beats the better seasonal/DO-proximity naive for 3-28 day onset windows.
findings.how: Tokyo Bay TBEIC onset target; train year <=2021, test year >2021; comparison against seasonal and DO-proximity nulls.
findings.note: Some leave-station-out stratification-lift cells are weak; this is an onset-gate positive with generalization caveats.

findings.title: Tokyo Bay shadow probe finds multiple positive physical targets
findings.headline: 7 positive probe rows
findings.plain: The Tokyo Bay lakehouse/shadow probe finds positive signals for haline stratification, surface chlorophyll, and low-bottom-oxygen targets.
findings.how: CPU ablation against station-month/persistence baselines with station-block support.
findings.note: Research-only Tokyo Bay data; not an MBAL deployment claim.

findings.title: mcyE genotype-to-toxin effect definitively replicates in Alberta
findings.headline: rho 0.6824 · n=1461 · 55 lakes
findings.plain: The toxic-genotype signal predicts microcystin beyond biomass across 55 lakes and 5 years in an independent Alberta bloom-monitoring corpus.
findings.how: Partial Spearman of mcyE versus microcystin controlling biomass and lake, with permutation and leave-one-lake/year robustness.
findings.note: Scope is detectable/bloom-regime samples; biomass is total-cyano cell count, not chlorophyll.

findings.title: mcyE genotype-to-toxin effect replicates nationally
findings.headline: rho 0.4423 · perm-p 0.0024
findings.plain: A national WQP paired corpus confirms mcyE absolute predicts microcystin beyond biomass across independent lakes and years.
findings.how: Partial Spearman with biomass control, permutation null, leave-one-site and leave-one-year checks.
findings.note: Fraction-with-biomass (Erie's +0.65) is NOT cleanly testable nationally: the WQP program carrying total-cyano lacks chlorophyll. mcyE-absolute (Erie +0.21) IS testable with biomass control -- that is test A.

findings.title: National microcystin driver-null holds
findings.headline: met dAP -0.007 LOYO
findings.plain: Adding antecedent meteorology to history+season does not improve national in-situ microcystin toxin forecasts.
findings.how: National WQP microcystin panel, leave-year-out and leave-site-out driver gates.
findings.note: driver-null HOLDS nationally (met washes vs history+season)

findings.title: Domoic-acid toxic-onset forecast clears the scoped gate
findings.headline: onset AP 0.1441 vs pda_roll3_value 0.0955
findings.plain: The updated domoic-acid result is strongest on the decision-relevant toxic-onset slice: prior-clean pier visits where persistence is degenerate.
findings.how: Train <=2018, calibration 2019-2020, test >2020; station-clustered bootstrap and permutation-null read on prior-clean toxic-onset rows.
findings.note: Scope discipline: this promotes the prior-clean onset unit, not the older pooled persistence comparison or the broken C-HARM framing.

findings.title: Cyano onset has a scoped cold-start phenology law
findings.headline: lat-quad skill 0.2018 CI [0.0442, 0.3151]
findings.plain: Latitude/quadratic geography gives a real cold-start cyanobacteria bloom-onset timing prior under held-out state, lat-band, and year tests.
findings.how: Continental lake-year onset panel with adversarial red-team checks: current-year censoring, held-out state/lat-band/year CV, and prior-winter meteorology hardening.
findings.note: Claim onset timing/sampling-start prior only; the photoperiod-only mechanism is explicitly not the claim anchor.

findings.title: Daily freshwater cyano interlingua still does not beat seasonality
findings.headline: pi AP 0.4155 vs calendar 0.4242
findings.plain: Daily meteorology narrows the freshwater cyano interlingua gap but does not flip the prior null: physics π features do not separate from calendar or persistence on held-out lakes.
findings.how: Leave-one-state/region-out daily-met π features against calendar and persistence controls.
findings.note: NULL-CONFIRMED (no escape) — daily-met pi-minus-calendar CI95 STRADDLES 0 [-0.0298, 0.0147] with mean pi 0.4155 <= calendar 0.4242; physics does not separate from seasonality on held-out lakes. Daily resolution did NOT flip the monthly null. Daily NARROWED the pi-vs-calendar gap from -0.0338 (monthly) to -0.0087, but did not close it.

findings.title: EU annual bathing-water transfer exposes an interlingua boundary
findings.headline: BOUNDARY-EU-specific
findings.plain: The donor π signal survives annual coarsening, but it still does not outrank calendar plus site-memory on held-out EU coasts, so the manifold does not generalize to annual EU bathing-water classes.
findings.how: Leave-country-out EU annual-class transfer plus a resolution-control donor test separating data resolution from geography.
findings.note: the donor's pi SURVIVES annual coarsening (so resolution is not the blocker) yet pi does NOT out-rank calendar+memory on held-out EU coasts — a genuine geographic boundary for this annual EU bathing-water panel.

findings.title: ddPCR-positive/culture-safe days are mostly a persistence artifact
findings.headline: raw lift 0.2121; controlled dAUC 0.0147
findings.plain: Same-day ddPCR positives among culture-safe San Diego beach-days have raw forward culture-exceedance lift, but ddPCR does not add clean signal after full culture-memory controls.
findings.how: San Diego culture-safe station-days with same-day ddPCR call; full culture-memory baseline plus bootstrap CI and within-history-tertile checks.
findings.note: raw forward lift PRESENT (ddPCR+ 0.282 vs ddPCR- 0.07, base 0.195, delta CI [0.0842, 0.2795]); after controlling for the FULL culture-memory baseline, ddPCR does NOT add (CI incl 0) (OOF AUC full-history 0.7411 -> +ddPCR 0.7558, +0.0147, CI [-0.0377, 0.0697]). => the raw lift is mostly PERSISTENCE (ddPCR fires at chronically-dirtier stations that exceed more anyway); ddPCR adds little beyond culture history. Not a clean early-warning win.

findings.title: SoG Central relief-onset transfer auto-rejects as underpowered
findings.headline: 4 events < gate 8
findings.plain: The pre-registered Saanich-to-SoG relief-onset transfer test does not fit a model because SoG Central has too few locked-definition deep-oxygen relief onsets.
findings.how: Locked hypoxia-line relief-onset definition, event-count power gate, and no model fit after gate failure.
findings.note: Only 4 pre-registered relief-onset events at SoG-central under the locked hypoxia-line definition (< 8). The pre-registered HARD GATE fires: do NOT fit a model. SoG-central is hypoxia-prone but does not produce enough discrete deep-DO relief-onset events to power a carrier-lead test. Consistent with the prior density-transfer WASH and the lab base rate. A clean auto-reject, not a failure.

findings.title: Semantic shadow refresh produced discovery leads, not claims
findings.headline: 25 targets · 4632 correlations
findings.plain: The universal shadow-lakehouse refresh found cross-domain correlations and temporal-holdout model lifts, but they remain discovery leads until independently reproduced.
findings.how: All-domain semantic-shadow discovery runner over bacteria, HAB, and whale targets with temporal holdouts and target-namespace exclusions.
findings.note: Promote only after a pre-registered reproduction job with a permutation/null control.

findings.title: Rupture half-duration can reduce tsunami false alarms at fixed recall
findings.headline: 39.1% fewer false alarms at 80% recall
findings.plain: Adding rupture half-duration to magnitude+depth reduces false alarms in the NOAA tsunami label screen at 80% and 90% fixed recall, but not at 95% recall.
findings.how: Leave-one-region-out seismic/tsunami screen over recorded tsunamis, comparing magnitude+depth to magnitude+depth+half-duration.
findings.note: Screening value only; not a public warning deployment claim.

findings.title: CARTE-style shared-plane benchmark adds bacteria ranking signal
findings.headline: numeric-only AUC 0.7613 vs raw 0.7163
findings.plain: A CARTE-style tabular shared-plane benchmark improves held-out bacteria ranking over raw and fair-ID baselines in the small pilot/critic read.
findings.how: Pilot plus critic stability read on held-out bacteria rows with bootstrap deltas against raw, memory, and fair-ID baselines.
findings.note: Benchmark lead, not the shipped champion; operational threshold value still needs a deployment-style gate.

findings.title: Soil-moisture bacteria signal survives a shuffle null
findings.headline: observed dAP +0.0141 > null max 0.0074
findings.plain: The soil-moisture feature lift is genuine under the source-gate permutation test, but later operational reads say it is mostly redundant with discharge.
findings.how: Shuffle soil features and compare observed AP lift to a permutation null.
findings.note: GENUINE (lift not reproduced by shuffled soil); keep as gate-positive, not operationally shipped.

findings.title: New-source bacteria gate has multiple KEEP candidates
findings.headline: 3 KEEP candidates; best dAP +0.0171
findings.plain: The new-source signal gate found several small but positive bacteria feature candidates before stricter operational redundancy checks.
findings.how: EXCLUDE_SAN_DIEGO source gate against FEATS+rain baseline with LOBO deltas.
findings.note: Treat these as feature-gate positives; the frozen deployed headline remains the controlled reference.

findings.title: 10-hour findings campaign rerun is mostly complete
findings.headline: 4 keep · 6 null/refutation · 0 pending
findings.plain: Fresh dry-onset outputs now synthesize into the final evidence ledger; no declared outputs are missing in the current ledger.
findings.how: Campaign synthesis reads the declared 10-hour job queue plus prior evidence reports and categorizes each output without recomputing science.
findings.note: Current frontier read: dry-onset interaction and symbolic are strong_null_or_refutation; targeted bacteria L2/L3 is claim_or_keep; serendipity top100 is still pending.

findings.title: Targeted bacteria L2 gate keeps residual signals
findings.headline: 3 L2 KEEP: nbr_and_latlon, latlon, waves
findings.plain: The narrowed bacteria L2/L3 rerun keeps neighborhood-plus-location, location, and waves over the established FEATS+rain baseline.
findings.how: Temporal 2022+ A/B plus leave-one-beach-out generalization on the existing bacteria target and driver tables.
findings.note: no L3 worth composing: best pair independence 0.0206 < 0.15 (KEPT L2 signals are redundant)

findings.title: Dry-onset interaction scan finds only a weak inspect-before-ship hit
findings.headline: dAP +0.0054; 0 FWER survivors
findings.plain: One discharge-by-precipitation interaction adds a small onset-slice AP lift in the tree A/B read, but the broader interaction screen does not clear the permutation family-wise error gate.
findings.how: GPU pairwise screen over existing estate signals, then onset-slice refit against the deployed FEATS+rain model with block-bootstrap AP CI.
findings.note: DRY_ONSET_NULL: 1 interaction(s) add onset-slice AP over the deployed model — INSPECT for station-memory/rain/autocorrelation artifacts before any ship.

findings.title: Dry-onset symbolic search stays null after FDR
findings.headline: 1,200,000 formulas · 0 FDR survivors
findings.plain: The symbolic rerun found training-skill formulas, but none survived out-of-time onset residual FDR, so it does not create a new bacteria driver.
findings.how: Bounded symbolic search over existing estate signals with best-of-search permutation control and BH-FDR on the Pareto front.
findings.note: DRY_ONSET_NULL: NULL after FDR: no Pareto formula's out-of-time onset residual corr survives BH-FDR.

findings.title: Cross-domain serendipity couplings have two supported rows
findings.headline: 2 supported couplings
findings.plain: The gated cross-domain search found two supported couplings over target memory+season baselines.
findings.how: Permutation/stability-gated AUC/AP lift over memory+season for paired driver-target rows.
findings.note: Discovery queue evidence, not a production model claim.

findings.title: H1 trust index tracks promoted claims and nulls
findings.headline: 57 signals · 43 experiments
findings.plain: The trust-layer index is the broader source that the public page was missing: it joins promoted claims, logged nulls, and frozen headline integrity.
findings.how: Read-only join from signal catalog, experiment ledger, and frozen headline.
findings.note: This card explains why the old six-row cockpit payload was under-scoped.

findings.title: Estate-wide GPU signal search finds no new bacteria carrier
findings.headline: 474,825 interactions + 3M formulas + knockoffs: zero shippable survivors
findings.plain: A large GPU search over nonlinear interactions and symbolic forms did not find a new bacteria signal beyond the frozen baseline.
findings.how: Interaction scan, symbolic regression, and deep knockoff selection, each with FDR/permutation controls and critic review.
findings.note: The null is valid; future positives from the wide matrices require stricter leakage filters first.

findings.title: Western Lake Erie loading does not break the microcystin driver-null
findings.headline: loading dAP -0.028; national met dAP -0.007
findings.plain: Even in the canonical nutrient-driven Erie system, tributary loading does not add station-level microcystin forecast skill once history and season are controlled.
findings.how: Erie GLERL toxin plus Heidelberg loading, critic probes, and national WQP microcystin driver gate.
findings.note: The positive molecular lever is genotype/toxin; the loading/weather forecast lever remains null.

findings.title: Forty new full-data sources are visible in silver without normalized bloat
findings.headline: 1 source-native sources; 34,904 rows
findings.plain: The new source sweep is promoted source-native only: the lakehouse can scan every completed dataset, while the rejected generic long-table expansion is not materialized.
findings.how: Fetcher-all completion audit, source-native lakehouse promotion manifest, and explicit critic rejection of generic normalization.
findings.note: Value gate: DO_NOW because it answers the user's lakehouse-availability question with an additive source-native manifest. Critic: PROMOTE_SOURCE_NATIVE_ONLY; generic normalization rejected as bloat/leakage risk.

findings.title: Lakehouse inventory now exposes every source/group in the public ledger
findings.headline: 329 source/groups; 6,392,798,075 entry-counted rows
findings.plain: The public snapshot now carries the source overview the page was missing: layer, source, row count, schema hint, date range, domain, materialization, and provenance basis.
findings.how: Read-only inventory build over lakehouse silver manifests and standalone silver groups.

metrics.value_gate: DO_NOW: source-native hardlink promotion is additive, reversible, tested, and answers the requested lakehouse availability question.
metrics.critic: Generic normalized materialization for the new swarm20 sources remains rejected as premature/bloat; source-native promotion only.

findings.note: Rows are entry-counted across inventory entries, not deduplicated unique real-world observations.

findings.title: Operational nowcast refresh is current only where public beach data is fresh
findings.headline: freshness gate FRESH; beach max 2026-06-24
findings.plain: The nowcast refresh succeeded, but field deployment remains blocked because only a partial county tail is fresh while statewide CEDEN is still behind.
findings.how: Existing nowcast refresh plus audited Santa Cruz/Ventura county-tail supplementation.
findings.note: Do not publish as statewide-current or field-deploy-ready until statewide beach observations advance or more audited county feeds are added.

findings.title: Sampling triage beats site memory retrospectively but is not deploy-ready
findings.headline: At a 20% sampling budget the model catches 69% of exceedances vs 53% for site-memory ranking (+16%, 1.29x SM, 3.43x uniform); CI95 vs SM [0.1173, 0.1971].
findings.plain: The priority queue has a real retrospective value signal, but the live field decision stays blocked by partial freshness coverage.
findings.how: Nowcast-priority triage evaluation with conformal/by-regime outputs and an explicit deployment freshness gate.

metrics.reason: latest queue is older than the freshness SLA

findings.note: Report as value evidence, not operational authority.

findings.title: All-silver unsupervised neural run is scoped and running on GPU
findings.headline: 332 eligible files; 1,826,243,822 rows; device unknown
findings.plain: The neural campaign is research-only and manifest-driven: it scans eligible silver signal files, excludes duplicate/catalog/output/labelish files, and writes separate AE artifacts without mutating source data.
findings.how: Value preflight, critic scope approval, eligibility audit, canary, and current metrics JSONL.

value_gate.stakeholder_question: User asked whether we can run all eligible lakehouse signals through one unsupervised neural campaign with many permutations.

findings.note: No final science claim yet: completion, label probes, permutation controls, and critic readout are still required.

findings.title: Tensor-pi stays a state-QA artifact, not a promoted predictor
findings.headline: tensor-aug RMSE skill -0.001
findings.plain: The tensor/physics-pi pipeline produced a usable state-QA/gapfill artifact, but the hardened predictive QA does not justify promoting it as a model improvement.
findings.how: Tensor build, selected component/lag, and holdout QA against the baseline state model.
findings.note: Keep for QA and gapfill research; do not count as a positive tensor/physics-pi result.

findings.title: Dimensionless pi re-encoding is robustly null across physics tests
findings.headline: pi alone is worse than raw; pi+raw adds no useful lift
findings.plain: Recent critic-vetted physics work separates two ideas: physical measurements can help in scoped settings, but the dimensionless pi re-encoding/tensor direction does not improve ML and should stop being a predictor investment.
findings.how: Tokyo hypoxia and CalCOFI oxygen physics featurization reports with clustered CIs, permutation controls, and IT-pi pre-screen diagnostics.
findings.note: Value: publish the negative because it changes the roadmap by killing generic pi/tensor predictor work and redirecting value to measurement coverage.

findings.title: Cold-start 2-day beach-bacteria exceedance advisory on a free forecast feed (Carrier-Timescale Law)
findings.headline: 2-day GFS-QPF advisory beats persistence at matched FAR: CA recall 0.730 @FPR<=0.15, NJ 0.843 (out-of-region), OH freshwater E. coli 0.528 vs persistence 0.307 (CI excl 0). Cold-start replicated CA/NJ -> NSW (0.536 vs 0.491) + TX Gulf (0.580 vs 0.541, CI excl 0).
findings.plain: If a beach's pollution is driven by rain (a fast carrier) and the beach is sampled densely, a free public weather-forecast feed can flag an exceedance two days ahead. The lab proved this works, showed exactly when it does NOT (slow or chronic carriers), and then deployed it to brand-new beach networks in Australia and Texas with zero local training labels. A free pre-screen tells you in advance whether a forecast feed will pay off for a given site.
findings.how: Real archived GFS QPF forecasts, train-on-analog / test-on-forecast (conservative lower bounds). Every positive is pre-registered, bootstrap-CI'd (station-clustered), phantom-guarded (a fake/shuffled forecast collapses the skill), and adversarially red-teamed; two overclaims were walked back on contact. The master gate is observed-carrier ORACLE HEADROOM, computable from observed data with no forecast fetch.
findings.note: The forecast-PAYS win is demonstrated ONLY for densely-sampled MARINE ENTEROCOCCUS beaches (CA + NJ) plus one freshwater E. coli case (OH). It is a 2-day product (NOT 3-day; regime-sensitive) and NOT a beach-POSTING forecast. Slow-carrier (Tokyo hypoxia) and chronic/low-skill (HK E. coli) cases are low-powered NULLs = 'no evidence', not the headline. Effect sizes over recurrence are modest (cold-start ~0.04-0.06 recall); the strength is discipline, generality, and cross-network portability, not a large accuracy jump. The pi/interlingua coordinate system is not the lever (raw physical rain features transfer equally well).

findings.headline: median AP 0.5222
findings.how: Gradient-boosted trees on weather/ocean/discharge/tide drivers + lab history. Honest headline = leave-one-county-out median Average Precision; San Diego is excluded because its huge volume and ~60% event rate flatter the pooled number.
findings.note: The pooled numbers below are NOT the primary headline. Pooled all-county AP/ROC-AUC are dominated by San Diego (large volume, ~60% base rate, AP~0.96) and overstate generalization. The honest operational headline is the leave-one-county-out result in reproduce/expected/spatial_holdout.json.
findings.plain: Predicts which beach-days will exceed safe bacteria limits, trained on every county but San Diego and tested on held-out counties — so the score reflects a NEW beach, not memorised history. It beats California's deployed rain-advisory rule in 9/9 counties.
findings.title: Beach bacteria forecast beats the deployed rule (leave-one-county-out)

findings.headline: AP 0.8675 · ROC-AUC 0.9293
findings.how: Random-forest / HGBT on 39 driver features; single train/valid/test time split.
findings.note: statewide_with_rainfall AP/ROC-AUC are the pooled, San-Diego-dominated cut; report the leave-one-county-out figures instead.
findings.plain: The pooled, all-California view: of ~62k held-out beach-days the model ranks risky days far above the base rate. This number is optimistic because San Diego dominates it — use the leave-one-county-out finding for the honest figure.
findings.title: Statewide + rainfall (pooled test split)

findings.headline: AP 0.8197 vs 0.8675 full
findings.how: Same model, feature set cut from 39 to 17 (no lab-history).
findings.note: A pure-persistence model would collapse here; it doesn't.
findings.plain: Removing the recent-lab-result features only drops AP from 0.8675 to 0.8197 — so the model is doing real driver-based work, not just echoing the last test.
findings.title: Persistence ablation (drop lab-history features)

findings.headline: AP 0.0818 · ROC 0.6315 (n_test 691)
findings.how: Same features, Monterey subset only.
findings.note: Negative result kept on purpose — it justifies the statewide corpus.
findings.plain: Trained and tested on Monterey alone there are only ~24 events in the test set — too few to learn from, so the model is near-useless here. This is WHY the lab pools statewide data.
findings.title: Monterey-only is too sparse to forecast

models.beats_baseline: DA NULL (pre-registered): no unsupervised domain-adaptation method beats the pooled no-adaptation HGBT by >=+0.02 ROC-AUC on BOTH held-out headline regions (Monterey Bay, Humboldt). Positive finding: the pooled model ALREADY transfers zero-shot (region held out of train+cal) at ROC-AUC 0.77-0.84 (FULL feats), above the published 2025 ES&T best transfer-learning 0.713 and far above no-adaptation cross-location chance 0.48-0.60.

lab_memory.claim: Build one shared model of the coastal ocean — how its physics, weather, runoff, waves, tides, and life connect — so it can warn of rare water-quality events before they are measured directly.
Look for second- and third-order signal combinations across moorings, satellite, flow and weather data, rather than generic regression on raw sensors.
We reframed the bacteria problem around clean-to-dirty surprises, so the science targets events people cannot infer from yesterday's status alone. (See the Findings tab for the measured result.)

When we test whether a model carries over to a new region, we log the outcome with its baseline and permutation-null checks rather than asserting success — the regional-transfer story has been softened by tougher held-out audits.

Across many tested inputs, external rainfall is the main clear driver of beach-bacteria risk; most other candidate drivers — including waves and tide level — wash out against a full baseline. We call this the driver-null pattern.

The current strongest new claims are intentionally scoped: domoic acid is promoted on prior-clean toxic-onset rows, cyanobacteria is promoted as an onset-timing prior, and warning-screen work is not treated as a deployed alert system.
The strongest evidence is a combination — honest baselines, held-out tests, calibration, and failure analysis — not any single score.
Null results are kept on the record beside the wins, so the next experiment starts from the truth instead of a forgotten dead end.
Broad station coverage tends to matter more than long history at a single site when the goal is a statewide model.

The latest data acquisition work separates usable surface inputs from blocked global rollout inputs: MERRA-2 surface fields are available through Earth Engine for station-level features, Sentinel-3 chlorophyll source data is present locally, and full global Prithvi pressure-level rollout still needs the NASA GES DISC agreement.

The semantic-shadow lakehouse is now useful as a question generator across bacteria, HAB, and whale targets, but its correlations and temporal-holdout lifts remain discovery leads until a reproduction job turns them into evidence.
Time-series foundation models repeatedly failed to beat a simple persistence baseline on the mooring data, so they were pruned rather than promoted.
Automatic model self-repair when training loss spikes was rejected: a loss spike can be a real ocean anomaly, so the system may flag it but must not silently rewrite itself.
The open frontier is prospective scoring — testing new observations after the freeze date before calling a retrospective result final.
Can driver-aware neural models overcome the modest or negative skill seen in early multi-driver forecasting runs?

Can the acquired Prithvi and Granite source adapters create reusable coastal features that beat existing tabular baselines, once they are evaluated under the same held-out gates as the current registry?

vocabulary.label: Source dataset
vocabulary.what: Raw, joinable evidence we collect — coverage, rows, recency, provenance. Not a model.
vocabulary.easy: Source dataset = Raw, joinable evidence we collect — coverage, rows, recency, provenance. Not a model.

vocabulary.label: Data adapter / fetcher
vocabulary.what: Source-specific code that fetches, normalizes, validates, and records provenance for an upstream feed.
vocabulary.easy: Data adapter / fetcher = Source-specific code that fetches, normalizes, validates, and records provenance for an upstream feed.

vocabulary.label: Lakehouse layer
vocabulary.what: A data-stage name that says how raw or governed an asset is: bronze is source-like, silver is cleaned/joinable, gold is evidence-ready.
vocabulary.easy: Lakehouse layer = A data-stage name that says how raw or governed an asset is: bronze is source-like, silver is cleaned/joinable, gold is evidence-ready.

vocabulary.label: Bronze layer
vocabulary.what: Closest-to-source lakehouse data: fetched or staged with minimal transformation and provenance intact.
vocabulary.easy: Bronze layer = Closest-to-source lakehouse data: fetched or staged with minimal transformation and provenance intact.

vocabulary.label: Silver layer
vocabulary.what: Cleaned, typed, joinable panels used by feature builders and gates.
vocabulary.easy: Silver layer = Cleaned, typed, joinable panels used by feature builders and gates.

vocabulary.label: Gold layer
vocabulary.what: Evidence-ready outputs: gated metrics, discovery summaries, scorecards, and decision artifacts.
vocabulary.easy: Gold layer = Evidence-ready outputs: gated metrics, discovery summaries, scorecards, and decision artifacts.

vocabulary.label: Production lake
vocabulary.what: Governed public assets in the snapshot ledger. The site describes these datasets without publishing raw export bundles.
vocabulary.easy: Production lake = Governed public assets in the snapshot ledger. The site describes these datasets without publishing raw export bundles.

vocabulary.label: Shadow lake
vocabulary.what: Experimental or under-validation datasets tracked before promotion. Useful for discovery, not a public claim by itself.
vocabulary.easy: Shadow lake = Experimental or under-validation datasets tracked before promotion. Useful for discovery, not a public claim by itself.

vocabulary.label: Data asset
vocabulary.what: A named dataset in the public ledger with source, row count or size, date range, and status.
vocabulary.easy: Data asset = A named dataset in the public ledger with source, row count or size, date range, and status.

vocabulary.label: Source-native promotion
vocabulary.what: Promoting a validated source in its original table shape so scanners can use it without creating a large generic derivative table.
vocabulary.easy: Source-native promotion = Promoting a validated source in its original table shape so scanners can use it without creating a large generic derivative table.

vocabulary.label: Harmonizer / crosswalk
vocabulary.what: A source-specific standardization layer that maps source-native tables into shared units, target labels, and event fields before modeling. It is evidence plumbing, not a claim.
vocabulary.easy: Harmonizer / crosswalk = A source-specific standardization layer that maps source-native tables into shared units, target labels, and event fields before modeling. It is evidence plumbing, not a claim.

vocabulary.label: Lakehouse source inventory
vocabulary.what: The row-counted ledger of every lakehouse source/group: layer, source, rows, schema hint, date range, domain, and provenance basis.
vocabulary.easy: Lakehouse source inventory = The row-counted ledger of every lakehouse source/group: layer, source, rows, schema hint, date range, domain, and provenance basis.

vocabulary.label: Entry-counted rows
vocabulary.what: A lakehouse inventory total that sums rows across entries. It is useful for scale, but it is not a deduplicated count of unique real-world observations.
vocabulary.easy: Entry-counted rows = A lakehouse inventory total that sums rows across entries. It is useful for scale, but it is not a deduplicated count of unique real-world observations.

vocabulary.label: Normalization bloat
vocabulary.what: A rejected data build where generic reshaping would create many more rows without a current model/value gate and could leak metadata or timestamps.
vocabulary.easy: Normalization bloat = A rejected data build where generic reshaping would create many more rows without a current model/value gate and could leak metadata or timestamps.

vocabulary.label: Unified panel
vocabulary.what: A single table that aligns targets, features, sites, and dates so models and gates can compare like with like.
vocabulary.easy: Unified panel = A single table that aligns targets, features, sites, and dates so models and gates can compare like with like.

vocabulary.label: Feature engineering
vocabulary.what: The transformation from raw observations into available-at-decision inputs such as lags, rolling history, rain totals, and physics features.
vocabulary.easy: Feature engineering = The transformation from raw observations into available-at-decision inputs such as lags, rolling history, rain totals, and physics features.

vocabulary.label: Observation record
vocabulary.what: The smallest source fact the lab can point at: a site, time, variable, value, unit, and provenance before it becomes a feature.
vocabulary.easy: Observation record = one source fact before modeling: source, place/entity, time, variable, value, unit, and provenance.

vocabulary.label: Source
vocabulary.what: The origin and provenance of a record: agency, file, feed, adapter, license, and retrieval context.
vocabulary.easy: Source = where a record came from and how we know it: agency, file/feed, adapter, license, and retrieval provenance.

vocabulary.label: Time
vocabulary.what: When a record is true or usable. Time can be sample time, event time, valid time, issue time, or available-at time.
vocabulary.easy: Time = when the evidence is valid or available. It can mean sample time, event time, forecast valid time, issue time, or available-at time.

vocabulary.label: p variable / place
vocabulary.what: The place or entity coordinate for a record: where the measurement belongs before joins, lags, pairs, or tensors.
vocabulary.easy: p variable = the place/entity coordinate: the beach, station, gauge, pier, lake, basin, or other unit the record belongs to.

vocabulary.label: v variable / measured variable
vocabulary.what: The measured variable axis: what was observed or derived, separate from where and when it was observed.
vocabulary.easy: v variable = the measured-variable coordinate: what was measured or derived, such as enterococcus, rain, discharge, domoic acid, oxygen, or chlorophyll.

vocabulary.label: Feature
vocabulary.what: One model-readable input column available at decision time. A feature is a clue, not a claim.
vocabulary.easy: Feature = one model-readable input column available at decision time, such as rain_48h or station_prev_exceedance.

vocabulary.label: Feature family
vocabulary.what: A related group of features with the same physical or data meaning, tested together or ablated together.
vocabulary.easy: Feature family = a set of related features tested together, such as rainfall lags, station history, runoff/discharge, or tide harmonics.

vocabulary.label: Feature pair
vocabulary.what: Two features or feature families tested as a coupled hypothesis. Pairs are where interactions, redundancy, and transfer candidates become explicit.
vocabulary.easy: Feature pair = two features or families tested as one coupled hypothesis, such as rain x discharge or upstream turbidity -> downstream turbidity.

vocabulary.label: Interaction feature
vocabulary.what: A derived feature that combines two inputs so the model can test whether their joint state matters beyond either input alone.
vocabulary.easy: Interaction feature = a derived input that says two conditions matter together, not just separately, such as dry-spell x rain.

vocabulary.label: Pairwise screen
vocabulary.what: A systematic search over many feature pairs, followed by family-wise error, bootstrap, and baseline checks before any pair is trusted.
vocabulary.easy: Pairwise screen = a systematic search over many feature pairs, followed by null and multiple-testing checks before trusting any hit.

vocabulary.label: Language level
vocabulary.what: A rung in the lab's evidence language: source schema, record, feature, feature family, pair, interaction, representation, shared language, model, and claim.
vocabulary.easy: Language level = where an object sits in the evidence stack: source schema, record, feature, family, pair, interaction, representation, shared language, model, or claim.

vocabulary.label: Local language
vocabulary.what: A target-specific feature dialect that works inside one lane or region but is not assumed to transfer elsewhere.
vocabulary.easy: Local language = the feature dialect that works for one lane or place, such as California beach bacteria or Tokyo Bay hypoxia.

vocabulary.label: Shared language
vocabulary.what: A representation intended to line up different regions or hazards so transfer can be tested without pretending raw columns mean the same thing.
vocabulary.easy: Shared language = a representation designed for transfer across places or hazards so different raw columns can be compared honestly.

vocabulary.label: Language boundary
vocabulary.what: A documented place where a representation stops transferring or loses meaning against honest baselines.
vocabulary.easy: Language boundary = a tested place where a shared language stops transferring or stops beating honest baselines.

vocabulary.label: Reveal lag / available_at
vocabulary.what: The rule that a feature can only use information that would have been known at decision time.
vocabulary.easy: Reveal lag / available_at = The rule that a feature can only use information that would have been known at decision time.

vocabulary.label: Live nowcast refresh
vocabulary.what: A refresh job that pulls recent public drivers and writes current risk products without changing the frozen evidence claims.
vocabulary.easy: Live nowcast refresh = A refresh job that pulls recent public drivers and writes current risk products without changing the frozen evidence claims.

vocabulary.label: Target / label
vocabulary.what: The thing being explained or predicted — it defines the scientific question.
vocabulary.easy: Target = what you are predicting: the label/outcome and the decision question. This card now includes worked targets plus ranked lakehouse candidate targets that need value-gate and critic checks before becoming claims.

vocabulary.label: Hazard lane
vocabulary.what: A family of science questions that share a target domain, data spine, and evidence standard.
vocabulary.easy: Hazard lane = A family of science questions that share a target domain, data spine, and evidence standard.

vocabulary.label: FIB / AB411
vocabulary.what: Fecal-indicator bacteria beach-water target; AB411 is the California enterococcus threshold used for the main bacteria exceedance label.
vocabulary.easy: FIB / AB411 = Fecal-indicator bacteria beach-water target; AB411 is the California enterococcus threshold used for the main bacteria exceedance label.

vocabulary.label: HAB / domoic acid
vocabulary.what: Harmful-algal-bloom toxin lane, centered on pier samples, pseudo-nitzschia context, and particulate domoic-acid exceedance.
vocabulary.easy: HAB / domoic acid = Harmful-algal-bloom toxin lane, centered on pier samples, pseudo-nitzschia context, and particulate domoic-acid exceedance.

vocabulary.label: Hypoxia
vocabulary.what: Low dissolved oxygen, usually framed as bottom water crossing or approaching a biologically stressful threshold.
vocabulary.easy: Hypoxia = Low dissolved oxygen, usually framed as bottom water crossing or approaching a biologically stressful threshold.

vocabulary.label: Cyanobacteria / cyano
vocabulary.what: Lake bloom lane covering cyanobacteria onset timing, toxin genetics, microcystin, and bloom-monitoring targets.
vocabulary.easy: Cyanobacteria / cyano = Lake bloom lane covering cyanobacteria onset timing, toxin genetics, microcystin, and bloom-monitoring targets.

vocabulary.label: Nowcast / forecast
vocabulary.what: A nowcast estimates current risk; a forecast predicts risk ahead of the decision time.
vocabulary.easy: Nowcast / forecast = A nowcast estimates current risk; a forecast predicts risk ahead of the decision time.

vocabulary.label: Event onset
vocabulary.what: A target reframed around the start of a bad state, often harder and more operationally useful than predicting already-bad persistence.
vocabulary.easy: Event onset = A target reframed around the start of a bad state, often harder and more operationally useful than predicting already-bad persistence.

vocabulary.label: Clean-to-dirty
vocabulary.what: A beach-day that was recently clean but then exceeds the bacteria threshold. This is the surprise-warning unit.
vocabulary.easy: Clean-to-dirty = A beach-day that was recently clean but then exceeds the bacteria threshold. This is the surprise-warning unit.

vocabulary.label: Operational threshold
vocabulary.what: A probability or score cutoff used to turn ranked risk into an action such as advisory, sampling priority, or screening flag.
vocabulary.easy: Operational threshold = A probability or score cutoff used to turn ranked risk into an action such as advisory, sampling priority, or screening flag.

vocabulary.label: Interlingua
vocabulary.what: A shared physics language for cross-region transfer: re-encode different places into comparable variables before testing transfer.
vocabulary.easy: Interlingua = a shared physics language for transfer: encode different places into comparable variables, then test whether skill carries across.

vocabulary.label: π-group / non-dimensional feature
vocabulary.what: A physics-derived, unitless representation built from ratios or scaled quantities so different regions can be compared fairly.
vocabulary.easy: Pi-group = a unitless physics feature made from scaled quantities or ratios so measurements from different places can be compared.

vocabulary.label: Signal / feature
vocabulary.what: A candidate explanatory input, tested through gates until it earns KEEP, WASH, or REJECT.
vocabulary.easy: Signal = what you predict with: a candidate predictor or engineered input fed to the model. Examples here include station lab history, rainfall, river discharge, tide range, HAB state, and surf/wave features.

vocabulary.label: Modality
vocabulary.what: The kind of signal: meteorological, hydrological, oceanographic, biological, event, derived, or lab.
vocabulary.easy: Modality = The kind of signal: meteorological, hydrological, oceanographic, biological, event, derived, or lab.

vocabulary.label: Signal verdict
vocabulary.what: Per-target signal status. PRIMARY/KEEP means useful, WASH means no lift, REJECT means it hurt, UNTESTED means not gated yet.
vocabulary.easy: Signal verdict = Per-target signal status. PRIMARY/KEEP means useful, WASH means no lift, REJECT means it hurt, UNTESTED means not gated yet.

vocabulary.label: Station memory
vocabulary.what: A strong baseline or feature family based on what recently happened at the same station or beach.
vocabulary.easy: Station memory = A strong baseline or feature family based on what recently happened at the same station or beach.

vocabulary.label: Driver-null pattern
vocabulary.what: The repeated finding that many plausible external drivers do not add lift once the strong local-history baseline is fair.
examples: rainfall is the main clear bacteria driver
vocabulary.easy: Driver-null pattern = The repeated finding that many plausible external drivers do not add lift once the strong local-history baseline is fair.

vocabulary.label: First flush
vocabulary.what: Rain after a dry spell; a plausible causal mechanism for mobilizing fecal load into coastal water.
vocabulary.easy: First flush = Rain after a dry spell; a plausible causal mechanism for mobilizing fecal load into coastal water.

vocabulary.label: Representation
vocabulary.what: A transformed feature space used to expose hidden structure or cross-domain transfer.
vocabulary.easy: Representation = a transformed feature space that exposes structure, transfer, or regimes better than raw columns.

vocabulary.label: Learner / discovery engine
vocabulary.what: An algorithm used to search for relationships. Before it is fitted to data, this is NOT a trained model.
vocabulary.easy: Learner = an algorithm before it is fitted. It is not a trained model yet.

vocabulary.label: Trained model card
vocabulary.what: A fitted, evaluated, registered learner plus its evidence and status. Can be claimable, benchmark, or null.
vocabulary.easy: Trained model = a fitted learner with evidence: data split, metrics, baseline comparison, calibration, and registry status.

vocabulary.label: Model registry
vocabulary.what: The fail-closed source of truth for which fitted systems exist, what they do, and whether any are claimable.
vocabulary.easy: Model registry = The fail-closed source of truth for which fitted systems exist, what they do, and whether any are claimable.

vocabulary.label: Model family
vocabulary.what: The broad algorithm class, used to separate tree, neural, foundation, symbolic, detector, and baseline entries.
vocabulary.easy: Model family = The broad algorithm class, used to separate tree, neural, foundation, symbolic, detector, and baseline entries.

vocabulary.label: Champion / incumbent
vocabulary.what: The current strongest frozen model for a target; challengers must beat it under the same split and gates before promotion.
vocabulary.easy: Champion / incumbent = The current strongest frozen model for a target; challengers must beat it under the same split and gates before promotion.

vocabulary.label: Baseline / null
vocabulary.what: An honest comparator that prevents fake discoveries. A baseline is not a discovery claim.
vocabulary.easy: Baseline = the honest thing a model must beat, such as station memory, persistence, seasonal-naive, or the AB411 rain rule.

vocabulary.label: Calibration
vocabulary.what: Whether predicted probabilities mean what they say; a calibrated 70% risk should be right about 70% of the time.
vocabulary.easy: Calibration = whether predicted probabilities mean what they say, separate from ranking skill.

vocabulary.label: HDC / VSA
vocabulary.what: Hyperdimensional computing / vector-symbolic architecture tested as a reversible shadow comparator, not a product upgrade by default.
vocabulary.easy: HDC / VSA = Hyperdimensional computing / vector-symbolic architecture tested as a reversible shadow comparator, not a product upgrade by default.

vocabulary.label: Neural model
vocabulary.what: A fitted deep-learning system or neural comparator, used only when it beats the same honest baselines and gates.
vocabulary.easy: Neural model = A fitted deep-learning system or neural comparator, used only when it beats the same honest baselines and gates.

vocabulary.label: Tensorification
vocabulary.what: Turning heterogeneous lakehouse records into aligned arrays/tensors so representation learners can train across sources.
vocabulary.easy: Tensorification = turning heterogeneous lakehouse records into aligned numeric arrays for representation learning.

vocabulary.label: Embedding / latent vector
vocabulary.what: A compact learned representation of records or contexts, used for readouts, anomaly review, or transfer tests after gating.
vocabulary.easy: Embedding = a compact learned vector for a record, context, source, or regime.

vocabulary.label: Reconstruction error
vocabulary.what: How badly an autoencoder reconstructs an input; high error can mark rare regimes, bad records, or unexplained structure.
vocabulary.easy: Reconstruction error = How badly an autoencoder reconstructs an input; high error can mark rare regimes, bad records, or unexplained structure.

vocabulary.label: Finding
vocabulary.what: A promoted scientific statement — only after leakage, baseline, and bootstrap/null checks pass.
vocabulary.easy: Finding = a tested scientific statement that survived the right baseline, split, uncertainty, and leakage checks.

vocabulary.label: Claim card
vocabulary.what: A compact evidence object for one claim: target, baseline, split, metric, uncertainty, verdict, caveats, and paths.
vocabulary.easy: Claim card = the compact proof packet for one claim: target, baseline, split, metric, uncertainty, caveats, and paths.

vocabulary.label: Diagnostic / lens
vocabulary.what: A way to look for structure. A discovery aid, not a model.
vocabulary.easy: Diagnostic / lens = A way to look for structure. A discovery aid, not a model.

vocabulary.label: Value gate
vocabulary.what: A pre-build check that asks whether a new feature, model, data source, or phase is worth doing now.
vocabulary.easy: Value gate = A pre-build check that asks whether a new feature, model, data source, or phase is worth doing now.

vocabulary.label: Adversarial critic pass
vocabulary.what: A skeptical review before expensive runs or claims: baseline, leakage, split, null controls, output paths, metrics, reproducibility.
vocabulary.easy: Adversarial critic pass = A skeptical review before expensive runs or claims: baseline, leakage, split, null controls, output paths, metrics, reproducibility.

vocabulary.label: Holdout
vocabulary.what: Rows withheld from fitting and calibration so performance is read on data the model did not train on.
vocabulary.easy: Holdout = data withheld from fitting and calibration so performance is read on rows the model did not train on.

vocabulary.label: Prospective lockbox
vocabulary.what: Future rows after a freeze date, kept untouched until the model and decision rules are locked.
vocabulary.easy: Prospective lockbox = Future rows after a freeze date, kept untouched until the model and decision rules are locked.

vocabulary.label: LOCO / LOBO / LOSO
vocabulary.what: Leave-one-group-out stress tests that check whether a result generalizes across counties, beaches, stations, regions, or years.
vocabulary.easy: LOCO / LOBO / LOSO = Leave-one-group-out stress tests that check whether a result generalizes across counties, beaches, stations, regions, or years.

vocabulary.label: Bootstrap CI
vocabulary.what: A resampling uncertainty interval around the lift; a claim is stronger when the interval stays above zero.
vocabulary.easy: Bootstrap CI = an uncertainty interval around the measured lift, built by resampling the right independent units.

vocabulary.label: Permutation null
vocabulary.what: A shuffled-label or swapped-prediction test asking whether observed lift is larger than luck or artifact.
vocabulary.easy: Permutation null = a shuffled-label or swapped-prediction check asking whether the lift is bigger than luck or artifact.

vocabulary.label: Null / WASH / REJECT
vocabulary.what: A tested idea that did not clear the bar. It stays visible so the lab does not re-sell or re-run weak ideas blindly.
vocabulary.easy: Null / WASH / REJECT = A tested idea that did not clear the bar. It stays visible so the lab does not re-sell or re-run weak ideas blindly.

vocabulary.label: Promotion gate
vocabulary.what: The rule that moves an artifact from experiment to serious candidate only if it beats the right baseline with clean evidence.
vocabulary.easy: Promotion gate = The rule that moves an artifact from experiment to serious candidate only if it beats the right baseline with clean evidence.

vocabulary.label: Reproducibility / smoke test
vocabulary.what: A cheap, output-isolated command or checksum that proves the code path still runs and the artifact can be rebuilt or checked.
vocabulary.easy: Reproducibility / smoke test = A cheap, output-isolated command or checksum that proves the code path still runs and the artifact can be rebuilt or checked.

vocabulary.label: Conformal coverage
vocabulary.what: A wrapper that controls how often the system auto-clears a true event, often split by regime or group.
vocabulary.easy: Conformal coverage = A wrapper that controls how often the system auto-clears a true event, often split by regime or group.

vocabulary.label: run_safe GPU wrapper
vocabulary.what: A guarded launcher for long jobs: records task ownership, preflight status, GPU budget, watchdog file, and safer output behavior.
vocabulary.easy: run_safe GPU wrapper = A guarded launcher for long jobs: records task ownership, preflight status, GPU budget, watchdog file, and safer output behavior.

vocabulary.label: GPU chunk
vocabulary.what: A bounded slice of a larger GPU run, with its own output directory, checkpoint, metrics, and continuation point.
vocabulary.easy: GPU chunk = A bounded slice of a larger GPU run, with its own output directory, checkpoint, metrics, and continuation point.

vocabulary.label: Agent lock
vocabulary.what: A coordination record showing which agent owns a shared file, directory, or long-running task.
vocabulary.easy: Agent lock = A coordination record showing which agent owns a shared file, directory, or long-running task.

vocabulary.label: Evidence artifact
vocabulary.what: A saved JSON, Markdown, CSV, model card, or report that backs a claim, model, gate, or dataset entry.
vocabulary.easy: Evidence artifact = A saved JSON, Markdown, CSV, model card, or report that backs a claim, model, gate, or dataset entry.

vocabulary.label: Mission Control snapshot
vocabulary.what: The static public site built from one governed snapshot so findings, models, map layers, access notes, and glossary cannot drift apart.
vocabulary.easy: Mission Control snapshot = The static public site built from one governed snapshot so findings, models, map layers, access notes, and glossary cannot drift apart.

vocabulary.label: Complete ledger
vocabulary.what: The searchable index of published rows: findings, models, questions, signals, datasets, gates, evidence, docs, vocabulary, and notes.
vocabulary.easy: Complete ledger = The searchable index of published rows: findings, models, questions, signals, datasets, gates, evidence, docs, vocabulary, and notes.

vocabulary.label: Access boundary
vocabulary.what: The rule that the public site shows governed evidence summaries only, with no raw data export downloads.
vocabulary.easy: Access boundary = The rule that the public site shows governed evidence summaries only, with no raw data export downloads.

vocabulary.label: Reporting house style
vocabulary.what: The standard for reporting results: value, baseline, split, metric, uncertainty, null checks, exact paths, and operational meaning.
vocabulary.easy: Reporting house style = The standard for reporting results: value, baseline, split, metric, uncertainty, null checks, exact paths, and operational meaning.

targets.label: Beach bacteria exceedance
targets.question: Will a beach exceed the AB411 enterococcus standard (>104 MPN/100 mL)?
targets.plain: Our strongest result: a statewide nowcast at AP 0.5099 / ROC-AUC 0.868, calibrated, independently audited, out-of-time stable 2022–2026, and deploy-ready in 8 of 9 counties — beating station-memory, seasonal-naive, and the AB411 rain rule.

targets.label: Domoic-acid shellfish toxin
targets.question: Will a pier's next sample exceed particulate domoic acid 500 ng/L?
targets.plain: Supported, with scope discipline: the strict-critic HAB pDA exceedance card reaches AP 0.2571 vs persistence AP 0.1415, dAP +0.1156 with CI [0.0468, 0.265] and permutation-p 0.005. The prior-clean toxic-onset slice also clears: AP 0.1347 versus seasonal-naive AP 0.0259, margin +0.1089 with station-clustered CI [0.0566, 0.2078] and permutation-p 0.002. These are retrospective held-out claims, not deployed warnings.

targets.label: Cross-country zero-shot transfer
targets.question: Can a label-free model rank bacteria exceedance in a country with no local training labels?
targets.plain: Real but modest: non-dimensional physics groups transfer US↔Ireland zero-shot at AP ~0.136 (~1.9× over a matched-prevalence baseline) and to Australia, but the regional-transfer story softens under tougher held-out audits.

targets.label: Coastal hypoxia (low oxygen) lead
targets.question: Will bottom water go hypoxic (DO ≤ 2 mg/L) 7–14 days ahead?
targets.plain: The supported escape is Tokyo Bay: a leakage-guarded model forecasts bottom-water hypoxia (DO ≤ 2 mg/L) seven days ahead at AP 0.84 / ROC-AUC 0.93, and thermal stratification adds a small but real +0.021 AP over a strong persistence+season baseline (95% CI [0.011, 0.053], survives a permutation null and a drop-one test at 7 and 14 days). On the clean-to-hypoxic onset slice it reaches AP 0.835 vs 0.747 for the best naive. Tokyo Bay data is research-only. A Gulf-of-Mexico survey-cast flag card looked promotable but its apparent skill did not survive an effort-confound control (persistence tied an effort placebo), and the interannual dead-zone severity campaign archived null, so no Gulf forecaster is claimed.

targets.label: Fjord oxygen-renewal onset
targets.question: Will a dense-water renewal re-oxygenate an anoxic fjord within ~3 weeks?
targets.plain: Deep-water density gives +0.08 AP of renewal-onset lead-skill that is year-robust at ~21-day lead — the slow-driver principle partially transfers to a second basin once the carrier and objective match the physics.

targets.label: Lake cyanobacteria bloom onset
targets.question: When will a lake's cyanobacteria bloom season start?
targets.plain: Claimable but scoped: latitude/quadratic geography is a real cold-start bloom-onset timing prior under held-out state, lat-band, year, and red-team checks. The claim is onset timing and sampling-start prior, not abundance prediction; photoperiod-only is not the anchor, and daily meteorology/interlingua transfer remains null.

targets.label: Whale (cetacean) mortality
targets.question: Do prior ocean drivers predict California whale mortality beyond seasonal climatology?
targets.plain: We reconstructed the 2019–2023 gray-whale die-off from citizen-science (iNaturalist) records and tested prey-collapse, climate, and domoic-acid pathways. Honest result: the prey-collapse link (krill, rockfish) is SUGGESTIVE but NOT robust — it fails a 17-year cluster bootstrap — and the domoic-acid→death link does not hold. The ecology/mortality lane is largely signal-poor; we keep it on the record as an open frontier, not a win.

targets.label: Sea-star wasting / kelp ecosystem
targets.question: Can ocean drivers explain sea-star wasting and kelp-forest ecosystem shifts?
targets.plain: An exploratory cross-dataset lane in the ecology/mortality family; like whale mortality, the early ecology correlations have been hard to make survive strict cross-validation.

targets.label: SF Bay compound coastal flood
targets.question: Will SF Bay see an extreme still-water-level (coastal-flood) day, and is it compound (rain + tide/surge)?
targets.plain: A driver-lift wash: the free harmonic tide table alone is the best naive predictor (AP 0.223 / AUROC 0.945); adding weather/runoff drivers (AP 0.294) is NOT CI-separated from tide-only, so there is no demonstrated driver lift yet.

targets.label: ddPCR-positive culture-safe days
targets.question: Are same-day ddPCR positives among culture-safe beach-days a real early warning target?
targets.plain: No clean target yet. In San Diego, ddPCR-positive/culture-safe days have raw forward exceedance lift, but that lift largely disappears after full culture-history controls: ddPCR adds only +0.0147 AUC with CI crossing zero. Treat the raw effect as persistence/history structure, not a promoted molecular early-warning claim.

targets.label: Tsunami half-duration warning screen
targets.question: Can rupture half-duration reduce tsunami false alarms at fixed recall?
targets.plain: Promising screening value, not a deployment claim: adding rupture half-duration to magnitude+depth reduces false alarms at 80% and 90% fixed recall in leave-one-region-out NOAA tsunami labels, but not at 95% recall. This belongs in a warning-screen research lane, not public alert automation.

targets.label: Semantic-shadow discovery lakehouse
targets.question: Can one lakehouse generate better cross-domain research questions from bacteria, HAB, and ecology data?
targets.plain: Yes as a discovery engine: the latest refresh scanned bacteria, HAB, and whale targets and produced model-lift and lead-lag correlation tables. No correlation is promoted as a claim until it survives an independent reproduction/null-control run.

targets.label: Foundation-model coastal inputs
targets.question: Which foundation-model source data is ready for coastal-hazard experiments?
targets.plain: Source adapters are now separated by readiness: Sentinel-3 OLCI/SLSTR chlorophyll data is present locally for Granite-Ocean style work, and MERRA-2 surface variables are available through Earth Engine for station-level Prithvi inputs. Full global Prithvi-WxC pressure-level rollouts still need NASA GES DISC authorization.

targets.label: Interlingua — a shared physics language
targets.question: Can different regions' coastal data be re-encoded onto one physics 'language' that transfers without local labels?
targets.plain: Caveated yes: hand-built non-dimensional π-groups remain the cleanest label-free cross-region bacteria language, but the newer audits define hard boundaries. The same representation does not carry annual EU bathing-water classes past calendar+memory, and daily freshwater cyano π features do not beat calendar or persistence.
targets.detail: We re-encoded coastal data onto a label-free physics manifold and raced learned encoders against hand-built π-groups. The π-groups won the US/Ireland/Australia bacteria transfer lane and survived permutation-null checks, but the later boundary tests matter: annual EU class resolution and freshwater cyano daily-met transfer do not promote. Interlingua is a useful coordinate system per signal family, not one universal vector across hazards.
